headline: DNA Structure
description: DNA (deoxyribonucleic acid) is the molecule carrying genetic instructions. Watson, Crick, and Wilkins (1953, Nobel 1962) - with crucial X-ray crystallography data from Rosalind Franklin - determined DNA's double helix structure: two antiparallel strands of sugar-phosphate backbone linked by complementary base pairs (A-T, G-C).

citation.name: The Double Helix: A Personal Account (2025 Edition)
citation.author: Watson J.D.

citation.name: DNA Structure and Function at 70: A 2025 Review
